name: Vishnu's Conch
description: Vishnu's Conch, also known as the Panchajanya, is a sacred conch shell held by the Hindu god Vishnu. It is a symbol of victory, auspiciousness, and the power of sound. Its sound, the 'Om', is believed to be the primordial sound of creation, resonating throughout the universe. The Panchajanya is not just an object but a divine weapon used by Vishnu to maintain cosmic order and defeat evil. The conch's unique spiral shape and its ability to produce a powerful, resonant sound contribute to its mystical significance. It is often depicted in Vishnu's hands along with other attributes like the Sudarshana Chakra, the mace (Kaumodaki), and the lotus flower (Padma). The sound of the Panchajanya heralds the arrival of Vishnu and is said to instill fear in the hearts of demons and inspire courage in the hearts of devotees. Its possession signifies divine authority and the ability to overcome adversity. The conch is an integral part of Hindu rituals and is blown during worship to invoke blessings and purify the environment. The Panchajanya is considered a powerful artifact with the ability to dispel negativity and promote spiritual well-being. Legends state that the conch was born from the ocean during the churning of the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthan), making it a celestial object of immense importance.
subtitle: Symbol of Victory and Divine Sound
origin: Born from the churning of the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthan)
currentLocation: Held by Vishnu in the spiritual realm

keyMyths.title: Kurukshetra War
keyMyths.description: Krishna blows the Panchajanya at the beginning of the Kurukshetra war, signifying the start of the battle and instilling fear in the enemy ranks.

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthan
keyMyths.description: The Panchajanya emerges from the churning of the cosmic ocean, solidifying its status as a divine object.

sources.author: F. Max Müller
sources.description: Collection of ancient Eastern religious texts
sources.text: Collection of ancient Eastern religious texts
